perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investigati on of complai nts or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which Companion Paws Canada and The LifeLine Canada Foundation must keep confidential as a result o f obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -relate d technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the exam inations and their dev elopment, maintenance and administra tion;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation e-mail distri bution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, con tractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Pollin g and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer or Training and Home Pr ovider provides to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer or Evaluator has access while involved in the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation activities.  Pictures and V ideos of Companion Paws dogs are only permitted without individuals unless the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation Waiver has been signed and initialed
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information only if clearly labelled as such:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investigati on of complai nts or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which Companion Paws Canada and The LifeLine Canada Foundation must keep confidential as a result o f obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -relate d technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the exam inations and their dev elopment, maintenance and administra tion;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation e-mail distri bution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, con tractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Pollin g and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer or Training and Home Pr ovider provides to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer or Evaluator has access while involved in the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation activities.  Pictures and V ideos of Companion Paws dogs are only permitted without individuals unless the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation Waiver has been signed and initialed
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the clause in the Non-Disclosure section that prevents divulging any Confidential Information to third parties without written consent. Because it limits the scope of Confidential Information, it now allows volunteers and evaluators to divulge certain information, like cost or polling data that isn't labelled, to third parties, which creates a conflict. This creates an **in-text contradiction** because the original text does not require this labelling, making all mentioned aspects confidential.
perturbation.location: Confidential and Proprietary Information section, paragraph 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure  Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator agrees not to, without prior written co nsent from Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , divulge any Confidential Information to third parties or copy documents containing any Confidential Information. In no event, shall Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator use Confidential Information i n a manner that is in any way detrimental to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition that Confidential Information is only deemed as such if 'clearly labelled as such.' The contradicted text states that the Volunteer agrees not to divulge any Confidential Information to third parties without prior written consent. The change introduces a scenario where certain information that was originally considered confidential is no longer confidential unless labelled, creating a direct conflict with the original non-disclosure agreement, as it now allows the divulgence of unlabelled information.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing , orally or by electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in th e course of service to C ompanion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation .
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information as explicitly defined and labelled as such and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing , orally or by electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in th e course of service to C ompanion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation .
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'as explicitly defined and labelled as such', this change contradicts the Code of Conduct requiring professional behaviour and respect. If unlabelled confidential information is disclosed, it could negatively impact stakeholders, violating the Code of Conduct, but would technically be allowed under the new definition of confidential information. This creates an **in-text contradiction** because conduct is a requirement for all companion paws volunteers, trainers and evaluators.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct section, paragraph 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the w ork and integrity of our organization and the services they provide, people will determine our reputation and abilit y to make an impact on the well -being of dogs and their handlers . The obligation of evaluators, trainers and volunteers to those we represent and serve will best be achieved thr ough a common Code of Conduct to guide our p rofessional practice. Therefore , compliance with this Code of Conduct is a requirement for all Companion Paws volunteer s, trainers and evaluators .
perturbation.justification: The change in the text adds 'as explicitly defined and labelled as such' to the definition of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which states that compliance with the Code of Conduct is a requirement for all volunteers, trainers, and evaluators. The Code of Conduct requires professional behaviour. If some confidential information isn't labelled as such, it would not be protected under the changed clause, which would allow a volunteer to disclose it even if it violated the Code of Conduct, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator further agrees that upon t ermination of se rvice with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, e lectronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation’s busine ss, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer or Evaluator further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator further agrees that upon t ermination of se rvice with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, e lectronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation’s busine ss, or in any way obtained during the course of service that is explicitly marked confidential. Volunteer or Evaluator further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Because the definition of 'Confidential Information' has been restricted to only explicitly marked information, the obligation to return 'all information' upon termination is now contradicted. Volunteers and trainers are now only required to return explicitly marked information, creating uncertainty about the handling of other sensitive data. This change introduces an **in-text contradiction** with the section outlining remedies for violating the agreement, as it becomes unclear what constitutes a violation regarding information return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies  Violation of this Agreement will be g rounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation . A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer or training and home provider at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by limiting the scope of 'Confidential Information' to only explicitly marked information. This directly contradicts the 'Remedies' section, which states that any violation of the agreement is grounds for termination. With the change, it becomes ambiguous what constitutes a violation since the obligation to return 'all information' is weakened, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding what information needs to be returned.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Companion Paws Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limite d to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest only in matters relating to explicitly marked confidential information pertaining to the Companion Paws Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limite d to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional relationships.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the conflict of interest clause to only matters regarding explicitly labelled confidential information. It introduces an **in-text contradiction** as it permits conflicts of interest pertaining to non-labelled confidential information, undermining the organization's integrity and reputation which are supposed to be ensured by volunteers.
perturbation.location: Volunteer or Evaluator Conflict of Interest section, paragraph 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct section, paragraph 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the w ork and integrity of our organization and the services they provide, people will determine our reputation and abilit y to make an impact on the well -being of dogs and their handlers . The obligation of evaluators, trainers and volunteers to those we represent and serve will best be achieved thr ough a common Code of Conduct to guide our p rofessional practice. Therefore , compliance with this Code of Conduct is a requirement for all Companion Paws volunteer s, trainers and evaluators .
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of conflict of interest avoidance to only matters relating to explicitly marked confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of the organization's integrity and reputation being upheld by volunteers, trainers, and evaluators through compliance with the Code of Conduct, regardless of whether the information involved is explicitly labelled confidential. The original text required avoidance of all conflicts of interest. The changed text now permits conflicts of interest as long as they don't relate to explicitly labelled confidential information, which creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If a situation arises where it is unclear as to whether a conflict of interest exists, Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall discus s the issue with the Chief Execu tive Officer of The LifeLine Canada Foundation.
perturbation.changed_text: If a situation arises where it is unclear as to whether a conflict of interest exists regarding explicitly labelled confidential information, Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator shall discuss the issue with the Chief Executive Officer of The LifeLine Canada Foundation only if the information is labelled confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the obligation to discuss potential conflicts of interest solely to explicitly labelled confidential information, this alteration creates an **in-text contradiction** with the volunteer's duty to act fairly and impartially. This creates an ambiguity: Volunteers are free to exploit unlabelled confidential information that could result in actions that are unfair and that do not benefit the parties, but, on the contrary, harms them.
perturbation.location: Volunteer or Evaluator Conflict of Interest section, paragraph 2
perturbation.contradicted_location: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct section, paragraph 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator shall act fairly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and to the population of Certificants and candidates.
perturbation.justification: The original text required discussion of any potential conflict of interest, while the changed text limits this obligation only to conflicts involving explicitly labelled confidential information. This contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that volunteers act fairly, impartially, and unbiasedly, because it now allows volunteers to potentially exploit unlabelled confidential information, leading to unfair or biased actions without requiring discussion or oversight. The modified text directly conflicts with the broader requirement of fairness and impartiality.
